SEARCH
Package com.societegenerale.failover.core.payload.splitter

Interface PayloadSplitter<T,R>

Type Parameters:
T - composite type — the type seen by the annotated method
R - slice type — the type stored/recovered per individual entry
All Known Implementing Classes:
AbstractListPayloadSplitter, AbstractPayloadSplitter
public interface PayloadSplitter<T,R>
Strategy that splits a composite payload into individual slices for scatter/gather operations.

Implementations are Spring beans registered with a qualifier or bean name matching Failover.payloadSplitter().

Author:
Anand Manissery
See Also:
  • ScatterGatherFailoverHandler

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    RecoverContext<T>
    merge(List<RecoverContext<R>> contexts)
    Merges individually recovered slice contexts back into a single composite result.
    List<RecoverContext<R>>
    splitOnRecover(RecoverContext<T> context)
    Splits the composite RecoverContext into per-slice recover contexts.
    List<StoreContext<R>>
    splitOnStore(StoreContext<T> context)
    Splits the composite StoreContext into per-slice store contexts.

  • Method Details

    • splitOnStore

      List<StoreContext<R>> splitOnStore(StoreContext<T> context)
      Splits the composite StoreContext into per-slice store contexts. Called once per failover store operation when a splitter is configured.
      Parameters:
      context - composite context carrying the full payload and original args
      Returns:
      ordered list of per-slice contexts to store individually

    • splitOnRecover

      List<RecoverContext<R>> splitOnRecover(RecoverContext<T> context)
      Splits the composite RecoverContext into per-slice recover contexts. Called once per failover recover operation when a splitter is configured.

      RecoverAll contract (findAll / no-ID-args scenario): When the method has no ID args, or Failover.recoverAll() is true, this method is called with the original (possibly empty) args. Each returned context is forwarded to FailoverHandler.recoverAll on the slice delegate — one store findAll call per context. Every context must set the correct slice type R via RecoverContext.clazz. Returning an empty list suppresses recovery and logs a warning.

      Note: When using the default DefaultFailoverHandler whose recoverAll ignores args and returns all entries by name, return exactly one placeholder context to avoid N-times duplication. Return multiple contexts only when the slice delegate's recoverAll partitions results by the supplied args.

      Parameters:
      context - composite context carrying the aggregate args and cause
      Returns:
      ordered list of per-slice contexts to recover individually; each context drives one recoverAll call on the slice delegate

    • merge

      RecoverContext<T> merge(List<RecoverContext<R>> contexts)
      Merges individually recovered slice contexts back into a single composite result. The returned context's payload field is the final recovered value.

      Deduplication: When splitOnRecover(com.societegenerale.failover.core.payload.splitter.RecoverContext<T>) returns N contexts in recoverAll mode and the slice delegate's recoverAll does not partition by args (e.g. the default DefaultFailoverHandler), all N calls return the same full set of entries — resulting in N-times duplicates in contexts. This method is the right place to deduplicate, since the implementation has full domain knowledge of the slice type R: 

      
 List<R> deduped = contexts.stream()
     .map(RecoverContext::getPayload)
     .filter(Objects::nonNull)
     .collect(Collectors.toMap(R::getId, r -> r, (a, b) -> a))
     .values().stream().toList();

      Null payloads: a context's payload may be null — a per-key cache miss (where the framework preserves positional alignment by passing the null through), an expired/missing entry on the recover-all path, or a slice that exceeded failover.scatter.timeout. This method owns the null policy: keep the null positionally (as the default per-id splitter does), or filter it out (as the recover-all dedup example below does). The framework only short-circuits to null when the entire contexts list is empty — in that case merge is not called at all.

      Parameters:
      contexts - per-slice contexts after each slice's payload has been recovered; payloads may be null, and the list may contain duplicates when multiple slices target the same store partition
      Returns:
      composite context whose payload is the merged result
